turns.assistant_text:
## Tóm tắt: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks

**Nguồn:** [Distill.pub](https://distill.pub/2021/gnn-intro/) (2/9/2021)

**Tác giả:** Benjamin Sanchez-Lengeling, Emily Reif, Adam Pearce, Alexander B. Wiltschko (Google Research)

---

### Nội dung chính

Bài viết giới thiệu về **Mạng nơ-ron trên đồ thị (Graph Neural Networks - GNN)**, gồm 4 phần:

1. **Dữ liệu dạng đồ thị** – Nhiều dữ liệu thực tế tự nhiên được biểu diễn dưới dạng đồ thị (tập hợp các **nút** và **cạnh** kết nối giữa chúng).

2. **Đặc thù của đồ thị** – Đồ thị khác biệt so với dữ liệu dạng chuỗi hay lưới (image), đòi hỏi các kỹ thuật xử lý chuyên biệt.

3. **Xây dựng GNN hiện đại** – Trình bày từng thành phần của mô hình GNN, từ các ý tưởng lịch sử đến các cải tiến mới, dần tiến tới mô hình state-of-the-art.

4. **GNN Playground** – Công cụ tương tác giúp người đọc thử nghiệm với dữ liệu thực tế để hiểu rõ hơn cách từng phần của GNN ảnh hưởng đến kết quả dự đoán.

### Ứng dụng thực tế
GNN đã được áp dụng trong nhiều lĩnh vực:
- 🔬 **Kháng sinh** – Phát hiện thuốc kháng sinh mới
- ⚛️ **Mô phỏng vật lý** – Mô phỏng vật lý phức tạp
- 📰 **Phát hiện tin giả** – Fake news detection
- 🚗 **Dự đoán giao thông** – Traffic prediction
- 🛒 **Hệ thống gợi ý** – Recommendation systems

---

*Bài viết liên quan:* [Understanding Convolutions on Graphs](https://distill.pub/2021/understanding-gnns/) – giải thích cách phép tích chập trên ảnh mở rộng sang đồ thị.

items.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
items.summary:
[Distill](https://distill.pub/)

A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks

# A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks

Neural networks have been adapted to leverage the structure and properties of graphs. We explore the components needed for building a graph neural network - and motivate the design choices behind them.

Layer 3

Layer 2

Layer 1

Layer 0

Hover over a node in the diagram below to see how it accumulates information from nodes around it through the layers of the network.


### Authors

### Affiliations

Benjamin Sanchez-Lengeling

Google Research

Emily Reif

Google Research

[Adam Pearce](https://roadtolarissa.com/)

Google Research

Alexander B. Wiltschko

Google Research

### Published

Sept. 2, 2021

### DOI

[10.23915/distill.00033](https://doi.org/10.23915/distill.00033)

_This article is one of two Distill publications about graph neural networks. Take a look at [Understanding Convolutions on Graphs](https://distill.pub/2021/understanding-gnns/)_

_- **Understanding Convolutions on Graphs**_

_A. Daigavane, B. Ravindran, G. Aggarwal._

_Distill. 2021._

_[DOI: 10.23915/distill.00032](https://doi.org/10.23915/distill.00032)_

_\[1\]_

_to understand how convolutions over images generalize naturally to convolutions over graphs._

Graphs are all around us; real world objects are often defined in terms of their connections to other things. A set of objects, and the connections between them, are naturally expressed as a _graph_. Researchers have developed neural networks that operate on graph data (called graph neural networks, or GNNs) for over a decade

- **The Graph Neural Network Model**

  F. Scarselli, M. Gori, A.C. Tsoi, M. Hagenbuchner, G. Monfardini.

  IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks, Vol 20(1), pp. 61--80. 2009.

\[2\]

. Recent developments have increased their capabilities and expressive power. We are starting to see practical applications in areas such as antibacterial discovery

- **A Deep Learning Approach to Antibiotic Discovery**

  J.M. Stokes, K. Yang, K. Swanson, W. Jin, A. Cubillos-Ruiz, N.M. Donghia, C.R. MacNair, S. French, L.A. Carfrae, Z. Bloom-Ackermann, V.M. Tran, A. Chiappino-Pepe, A.H. Badran, I.W. Andrews, E.J. Chory, G.M. Church, E.D. Brown, T.S. Jaakkola, R. Barzilay, J.J. Collins.

  Cell, Vol 181(2), pp. 475--483. 2020.

\[3\]

, physics simulations

- **Learning to simulate complex physics with graph networks**

  A. Sanchez-Gonzalez, J. Godwin, T. Pfaff, R. Ying, J. Leskovec, P.W. Battaglia.

   2020\.

\[4\]

, fake news detection

- **Fake News Detection on Social Media using Geometric Deep Learning**

  F. Monti, F. Frasca, D. Eynard, D. Mannion, M.M. Bronstein.

   2019\.

\[5\]

, traffic prediction

- **Traffic prediction with advanced Graph Neural Networks**

  O.L. \*, L. Perez. undefined.

\[6\]

 and recommendation systems

- **Pixie: A System for Recommending 3+ Billion Items to 200+ Million Users in {Real-Time}**

  C. Eksombatchai, P. Jindal, J.Z. Liu, Y. Liu, R. Sharma, C. Sugnet, M. Ulrich, J. Leskovec.

   2017\.

\[7\]

.

This article explores and explains modern graph neural networks. We divide this work into four parts. First, we look at what kind of data is most naturally phrased as a graph, and some common examples. Second, we explore what makes graphs different from other types of data, and some of the specialized choices we have to make when using graphs. Third, we build a modern GNN, walking through each of the parts of the model, starting with historic modeling innovations in the field. We move gradually from a bare-bones implementation to a state-of-the-art GNN model. Fourth and finally, we provide a GNN playground where you can play around with a real-word task and dataset to build a stronger intuition of how each component of a GNN model contributes to the predictions it makes.

To start, let’s establish what a graph is. A graph represents the relations ( _edges_) between a collection of entities ( _nodes_).

V

Vertex (or node)
